articles.title: CLOUD — START FOR FREE
articles.summary: Need more compute power (and choices) to fine-tune your generative AI? Intel® Developer Cloud [5] accelerates your AI development with Intel®-optimized software on the latest Intel® Xeon® processors and GPU compute.

articles.title: Start on the free tier to explore the latest Intel® AI tools
THE OPENAI KEYNOTE
articles.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman showcased new features and improvements to the company’s AI models at its first developer conference, emphasizing a future where AI integration will play a central role in consumer technology. The event highlighted OpenAI's shift towards a product-centric approach, with new tools being made immediately available, and hinted at potential forays into hardware to reduce friction in AI accessibility.

articles.title: NVIDIA IS PILOTING A GENERATIVE AI FOR ITS ENGINEERS
articles.summary: Nvidia revealed the testing of a large language model named ChipNeMo to enhance chip designers' productivity during a keynote at the IEEE/ACM International Conference on Computer-Aided Design. Although not yet fully proven, ChipNeMo aids in scripting for design tools, summarizing bug reports, and serving as an experienced chatbot for designers.

articles.title: YOUTUBE TO TEST GENERATIVE AI FEATURES
articles.summary: YouTube is testing new generative AI features as part of its premium subscription service. These features include a conversational tool for asking questions about content and getting recommendations and another for summarizing comment topics.

articles.title: TRANSFORMER BASED DIFFUSION MODEL IS 90% CHEAPER TO TRAIN THAN UNET BASED MODELS
articles.summary: PixArt is a new text to image model that uses T5 text encodings, cross attention, and a diffusion transformer to great results at a fraction of the compute cost of comparable models.

articles.title: ENHANCING LANGUAGE MODELS WITHOUT RETRAINING
articles.summary: The DARE method streamlines the enhancement of language models like BERT, allowing the integration of new functions into a unified model, increasing efficiency on various linguistic tasks.

articles.title: EFFICIENT LEARNING FOR SENTENCE EMBEDDING
articles.summary: Cross-View Training (SCT) elevates small language models, enabling them to produce sentence embeddings previously only achievable by larger models, optimizing both performance and computational efficiency.

articles.title: COGVLM-17B OPEN VISION LANGUAGE MODEL
articles.summary: With 10B vision parameters and 7B language parameters, this multimodal model excels at many standard benchmarks and performs well on human evaluation.

articles.title: LANGROID MULTI-AGENT PROGRAMMING FRAMEWORK
articles.summary: Inspired by the Actor framework, this lightweight Python library makes it easy to create LLM-powered agents.

articles.title: INSTANT 3D SEARCH
articles.summary: OVIR-3D innovates 3D object retrieval from text prompts using 2D image fusion, bypassing the need for 3D data training and facilitating instant, real-time search capabilities ideal for robotics.

articles.title: ALMOST AN AGENT: WHAT GPTS CAN DO
articles.summary: OpenAI's latest GPTs edge closer to the vision of autonomous AI agents. They are capable of executing tasks like crafting academic papers with minimal human input, despite current constraints like occasional inaccuracies. This leap forward in AI autonomy promises significant advancements but also underscores the urgency for vigilant development to mitigate potential vulnerabilities as AI begins to navigate more complex and independent roles.

articles.title: ARE LANGUAGE MODELS GOOD AT MAKING PREDICTIONS?
articles.summary: In a study using 5000 questions from Manifold Markets, GPT-4 was found to be consistently overconfident in its predictions.

articles.title: OPENAI IS A LOT MORE VULNERABLE THAN YOU THINK
articles.summary: On the heels of OpenAI releasing functionality that decimated half GPT-wrapper companies, here is a contrarian view of the blind spots and weaknesses of OpenAI as this market continues to evolve.

articles.title: AUTOMATIC SPORTS NARRATION (JUPYTER NOTEBOOK)
articles.summary: This notebook uses many new technologies from OpenAI to convincingly narrate a sporting event.

articles.title: AI NEGOTIATES CONTRACT FOR THE FIRST TIME EVER
articles.summary: British AI firm Luminance has developed an AI system that can autonomously negotiate contracts, aiming to streamline the workload of lawyers by handling routine negotiations.

articles.title: ORBA (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Convert your web visitors with personalized AI conversations.
